stack: Rust workspace (Axum 0.8, reqwest 0.12, jsonwebtoken 9) integrating flint-forge Quarry HTTP
budget_note: Light phase: integration against a known contract, no new runtime deps. Only external question was the test-mock crate.

gaps.gap: HTTP client for fpa-forge (call forge Quarry /openapi.json + /graphql)

candidates.name: reqwest
candidates.evidence: Already used by fpa-gate/fpa-mcp adapters and matches flint-gate's own client stack. No new dependency; add reqwest to fpa-forge and reuse the workspace pin.

candidates.name: hand-built JSON GraphQL body
candidates.evidence: Forge's /graphql body is standard { query, variables, operationName }. A typed graphql client crate (graphql_client) requires codegen against a schema and is overkill for a handful of read queries. Build the JSON body with serde_json; reconsider graphql_client if query volume grows.
candidates.notes: Matches forge's own GraphQlRequest domain shape.

gaps.gap: HTTP mocking for fpa-forge integration tests (Q1: fixtures vs live forge)
requirements: testability without a live Postgres-backed forge

candidates.name: wiremock
candidates.evidence: Async/tokio-native HTTP mock, matches our #[tokio::test] adapters. flint-gate already uses it to test its reqwest HTTP client — the closest sibling precedent to fpa-forge. Lets us test fpa-forge against recorded /openapi.json + /graphql responses without running forge.
candidates.notes: Alternative httpmock 0.8 is used by fabric's Ory adapters; either works, but wiremock aligns with gate's client-testing precedent and is async-first.

candidates.name: httpmock
candidates.evidence: Used by fabric's frf-identity-ory / frf-authz-keto. Also viable; wiremock preferred for async-native + gate precedent.

gaps.gap: Credential threading (forward gate bearer to forge) — CROSS-CUTTING

candidates.name: extend AuthContext with a raw bearer (internal design, no dep)
candidates.evidence: No library solves this — it is a port-signature design decision. Recommend adding an optional raw `bearer` to fpa-app::AuthContext, retained from the c001 gate extractor, and passing it through TaskRunner::run to the forge port method. Touches fpa-app + fpa-ports + fpa-forge + both surfaces.
candidates.notes: OPEN (Q2 from assessment): AuthContext.bearer vs a separate request-context object. Decide at spec. Never log the bearer (extend c003 audit-skip to the forge adapter).

candidates.name: std HashMap behind Arc<RwLock> (or tokio RwLock)
candidates.evidence: In-memory, single-process this phase; no store crate warranted (YAGNI). Use tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<TaskId, TaskRecord>> in the app layer. Durable/persisted store is a later concern (revisit when forge or a queue backs it).

key_adopt_verdicts: reqwest (already present) for fpa-forge HTTP — no new runtime dep
wiremock 0.6 as dev-dep for fpa-forge tests (matches flint-gate precedent, async-native)
Build GraphQL body with serde_json (no graphql_client codegen)
Credential threading + task store are internal BUILD decisions, not adopt (design at spec)
